description: This is a **Healthedata1** sandbox for creation of resources and examples

resource.description: This is a Clinician Authored SOAP Note example for the *US Core DocumentReference*. It demonstrates the "clinical-note" document category.
resource.name: Clinician Authored SOAP Note

resource.description: This is a Continuity of Care Document (CCD) example for the *US Core DocumentReference*. It demonstrates the "structured-document" document category.
resource.name: Continuity of Care Document (CCD)

resource.description: This is a International Patient Summary (IPS) example for the *US Core DocumentReference*. It demonstrates the "structured-document" document category.
resource.name: International Patient Summary (IPS)

resource.description: This is a Scanned Consent Form example for the *US Core DocumentReference*. It demonstrates the "scanned-document" document category.
resource.name: Scanned Consent Form

resource.name: Scanned Insurance Card

resource.description: This is a Scanned Photo ID example for the *US Core DocumentReference*. It demonstrates the "scanned-document" document category.

resource.description: This is a Scanned Wound Image example for the *US Core DocumentReference*. It demonstrates the "scanned-document" document category.
resource.name: Scanned Wound Image

resource.description: This Section describes the expected capabilities of the US Core Client which is responsible for creating and initiating the queries for information about an individual patient. The complete list of FHIR profiles, RESTful operations, and search parameters supported by US Core Servers are defined in the [Conformance Requirements for Server](CapabilityStatement-us-core-server.html). US Core Clients have the option of choosing from this list to access necessary data based on their local use cases and other contextual requirements.
resource.name: US Core Client CapabilityStatement

resource.description: This Section describes the expected capabilities of the US Core Client which is responsible for creating and initiating the queries for information about an individual patient. The complete list of FHIR profiles, RESTful operations, and search parameters supported by US Core Servers are defined in the [Conformance Requirements for Server](CapabilityStatement-us-core-server.html). US Core Clients have the option of choosing from this list to access necessary data based on their local use cases and other contextual requirements.
resource.name: US Core Client CapabilityStatement2

resource.description: This Section describes the expected capabilities of the US Core Server actor which is responsible for providing responses to the queries submitted by the US Core Requestors. The complete list of FHIR profiles, RESTful operations, and search parameters supported by US Core Servers are defined. Systems implementing this capability statement should meet the ASTP 2015 Common Clinical Data Set (CCDS) access requirement for Patient Selection 170.315(g)(7) and Application Access - Data Category Request 170.315(g)(8) and the ASTP [U.S. Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) Version 6 July 2025](https://www.healthit.gov/isp/sites/isp/files/2025-07/USCDI-Version-6-July-2025.pdf).
resource.name: US Core Server CapabilityStatement

resource.description: This Section describes the expected capabilities of the US Core Server actor which is responsible for providing responses to the queries submitted by the US Core Requestors. The complete list of FHIR profiles, RESTful operations, and search parameters supported by US Core Servers are defined. Systems implementing this capability statement should meet the ASTP 2015 Common Clinical Data Set (CCDS) access requirement for Patient Selection 170.315(g)(7) and Application Access - Data Category Request 170.315(g)(8) and the ASTP [U.S. Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) Version 6 July 2025](https://www.healthit.gov/isp/sites/isp/files/2025-07/USCDI-Version-6-July-2025.pdf).
resource.name: US Core Server UsCoreServerCapabilityStatement2
